title: The reflection seismic survey of project TIPTEQ—the inventory of the Chilean subduction zone at 38.2° S
abstract: This study presents findings from the reflection seismic component of the TIPTEQ (from The Incoming Plate to mega-Thrust EarthQuake processes) active-source seismology experiment across the Chilean subduction zone at 38.2°S. The seismic data clearly show the subducted Nazca plate with varying reflectivity along the plate interface. The researchers interpret high reflectivity at the plate interface as evidence of a subduction channel with thickness variations of 2–5 km. The survey acquired both marine MCS reflection profiles offshore and an onshore reflection component as part of the amphibious TIPTEQ experiment.

location.description: Offshore central Chile at 38.2°S, TIPTEQ profile across the subduction zone

seismic_lines.name: TIPTEQ NVR onshore reflection profile (~38.2°S) — 105 km CDP line, 73 NVR shots at 1.5 km spacing, Jan 2005

data.name: TIPTEQ Marine Multichannel Seismic Reflection Data
data.description: ONSHORE near-vertical reflection (NVR) profiles from TIPTEQ experiment at 38.2°S (Jan 2005), imaging the subduction zone to 50–60 km depth. Explosive borehole shots (75–300 kg), 180 three-component geophones at 100 m spacing, 8-fold CDP coverage. Kirchhoff prestack depth migration applied using SPOC velocity model. Reveals subduction channel at 2–5 km thickness.

processing.summary: Marine multichannel seismic reflection data from R/V SONNE cruise SO181 (TIPTEQ, 2004–2005) processed to image the full subduction zone at 38.2°S. The processing resulted in stacked and migrated sections that clearly image the subducted Nazca plate, subduction channel, and forearc structure. High-reflectivity zones at the plate interface are interpreted as the subduction channel with 2–5 km thickness variations.
processing.notes: Part of the international TIPTEQ (from The Incoming Plate to mega-Thrust EarthQuake processes) project. Amphibious survey including offshore and onshore reflection components.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF (open access, GJI 172:565-571). This paper is about the ONSHORE NVR reflection survey component of TIPTEQ, NOT marine MCS. vessel corrected: 'R/V SONNE' → null (onshore survey). source_type corrected: 'airgun array' → 'explosive shots' (75–300 kg borehole charges). year_acquired corrected: 2004 → 2005 (Jan 13–30, 2005). sample_rate_ms corrected: 2 → 5 (Table 1). channel_count: null → 180 per spread (EarthDataLogger PR6-24, 3-component, 4.5 Hz, 100m spacing). record_length_s: null → 102. Profile: 105-km CDP line, 8-fold NVR + ESP. Kirchhoff PSDM using Buske (1999) method with SPOC velocity model. Recording format MiniSEED.
